lib.load_data¶

Classes

CreateDataLoader(hitfinder_dataset, batch_size)

Data(*args, **kwargs)

class lib.load_data.CreateDataLoader(hitfinder_dataset, batch_size)[source]¶

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • hitfinder_dataset (Data)

  • batch_size (int)

__init__(hitfinder_dataset, batch_size)[source]¶

Takes a Data object as an input and uses DataLoader to split data into training and testing, or into running. Also includes getters :param hitfinder_dataset: dataset to be processed :type hitfinder_dataset: Data :param batch_size: batch size to split image data based on

Parameters:

  • hitfinder_dataset (Data)

  • batch_size (int)

Return type:

None

get_run_data_loader()[source]¶

Returns the run data loader for putting through the trained model.

Return type:

torch.utils.data.DataLoader

get_training_data_loaders()[source]¶

Get the training and testing data loaders.

Return type:

tuple

run_data_loader()[source]¶

Puts the run data into a dataloader for batch processing.

Return type:

None

split_training_data()[source]¶

Split the data into training and testing datasets and create data loaders for them.

Return type:

None

class lib.load_data.Data(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Parameters:

  • file_list (list)

  • executing_mode (str)

__init__(file_list, executing_mode)[source]¶

Initialize the Data object with classification and attribute data.

Parameters:

  • file_list (list) – list of paths to every data file being run

  • mode (executing) – Indicates whether hitfinder is in training or running mode

  • executing_mode (str)

Return type:

None

graph_image(array)[source]¶

Plot an image to see what an example of the data looks like to check orientation

Parameters:

array (nd.array) – Data that will be graphed
